@graph.name: ClinicalNotes-DeID
@graph.summary: De-identified clinical notes from electronic health records for clinical NLP model development.
@graph.description: A corpus of 180,000 de-identified clinical notes (discharge summaries, progress notes, radiology reports) from a tertiary care hospital system, covering 2015-2022. Notes span 15 specialty departments. Annotated for named entity recognition (NER) covering diseases, medications, procedures, and dosages. Collected and de-identified under IRB approval. Data use restricted to non-commercial clinical NLP research.
@graph.software_copyrightText: Copyright 2024 Clinical NLP Research Consortium and Contributing Hospitals
@graph.dataset_dataCollectionProcess: Clinical notes extracted from the hospital EHR system under IRB protocol #2022-0841. Only notes from adult patients (18+) with written informed consent or covered by institutional waiver for de-identified data. Extracted notes span encounters from January 2015 to December 2022. NER annotations created by 6 clinical informatics specialists using BRAT annotation tool.
dataset_anonymizationMethodUsed: Rule-based PHI detection covering 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifier categories
NLP-based residual PHI detection using i2b2 de-identification system
Manual review by clinical informatics specialist for all outputs
Re-identification attack simulation verified <0.1% re-identification rate on held-out set
dataset_dataPreprocessing: PHI de-identification using two-stage automated pipeline followed by manual review
Structured fields (ICD codes, medication orders) separated from free-text narrative
dataset_knownBias: Single-institution dataset; clinical practices and patient demographics specific to one urban academic medical center
Conditions with low prevalence in this patient population are underrepresented
Notes authored by residents may have different linguistic patterns than attending-authored notes
@graph.dataset_intendedUse: Development and evaluation of clinical NLP models for named entity recognition, relation extraction, and clinical text summarization. Not intended for clinical decision support without additional validation studies.
@graph.comment: SPDX 3.0 NOTE: This example demonstrates 'dataset_hasSensitivePersonalInformation: yes' and 'dataset_confidentialityLevel: amber' (TLP AMBER - restricted to consortium members and approved requesters). The 'dataset_anonymizationMethodUsed' property documents the de-identification methodology. Also: 'dataset_datasetSize' and 'dataset_intendedUse' are DEPRECATED in SPDX 3.1.
Data Use Agreement required. Non-commercial research use only. Access restricted to IRB-approved research projects. See https://example.com/datasets/clinicalnotes-deid/dua
